# HackXperience 2026

> SIM IT Club's flagship 2-day agentic hackathon. Theme: **AI for Living**. Teams of 3–4 build and deploy agentic products at SIM Campus, 24–25 July 2026.

## About
HackXperience 2026 is a student-run hackathon organised by SIM IT Club (SIMITC), a student-led technology club under SIM Student Life at Singapore Institute of Management. This year's edition is a **2-day agentic hackathon** where curious students build autonomous AI products around the theme **AI for Living** — easing the tension between work and life through care that nurtures wellbeing, or automation that brings flow back into the workday.

## Event Details
- Dates: 24–25 July 2026
- Duration: 2 days (agentic hackathon; non-overnight format)
- Registration deadline: 16 July 2026, 23:59 SGT
- Project submission deadline: 25 July 2026, 12:00 PM SGT
- Venue: Singapore Institute of Management (SIM), 461 Clementi Road, Singapore 599491
- Organiser: SIM IT Club
- Expected participants: 100+ students
- Team size: 3–4 members (one Team Leader as primary contact)
- Cross-university teams: Allowed
- Registration fee: Free
- Contact: it@mymail.sim.edu.sg
- Telegram community (SIM ITCommunity): https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl
- Telegram contact: @FukutaroJFS
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/simitclub
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/sim-information-technology-club/
- Website: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app

## Registration
- Team registration (Team Leader submits for the whole team): https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/BjC48ET3vW
- Looking for a team (solo or incomplete teams): https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/Lycc7m85fx
- Registration closes 16 July 2026, 23:59 SGT

## Pre-Events
- **Building Agentic AI: Microsoft Foundry Workshop** — 17 July 2026, 19:00–22:00, SIM Campus. Covers agent fundamentals, Microsoft Foundry ecosystem, multi-agent workflows, hands-on agent builds, and responsible AI. Room details via Telegram.

Pre-events are optional but highly recommended.

## Theme & Tracks
Theme: **AI for Living** — how can AI help us live better, not just keep up?

Teams choose one of two main tracks. Sub-tracks guide builds and are selected at project submission.

### Care Forward
Wellbeing across mind and body — reimagine care in daily living.
- **1A Mental Care** — social apps, mental wellness tools, caregiver coordination
- **1B Physical Care** — fitness tracking, training/endurance apps, exercise routines
- **1C Nutrition & Diet** — food label scanning, nutrition trackers, meal planning

### Friction To Flow
Reduce workday friction with agentic AI.
- **2A Task & Time Management** — task allocation agents, calendar agents, meeting summarizers, focus assistants
- **2B Work Quality & Integrity** — plagiarism/AI checkers, citation assistants, code review agents, career coaching
- **2C Workflow Automation** — admin automation, cross-app connectors, document generation, personal chief-of-staff agents

## Prizes
Total prize pool: $1,800+

| Award | Prize |
|---|---|
| Care Forward Track — Winner | $300 |
| Care Forward Track — Runner-up | $150 |
| Friction To Flow Track — Winner | $300 |
| Friction To Flow Track — Runner-up | $150 |
| Best Use of Microsoft Stack (sponsor choice) | $700 ($200 cash + Microsoft Certification access worth SGD $500) |
| Best Entrepreneurial Award (sponsor choice) | $100 |
| Community Choice Award (participant voting) | $50 |
| Game Prize (informal, details TBC) | $50 |

Judging criteria emphasise creativity & impact, agentic depth (autonomous reasoning, tool use, orchestration), technical execution, and presentation quality. Track-specific criteria are on the website Prizes section.

## Judges
- **Vincent Choy** — Senior Cloud Consultant, Fedelelis · Microsoft MVP
- **Richard Lee** — Chief Architect, IntelWave AI
- **Cher Lim** — AI Educator, SIM Global Education

## Sponsors & Partners
- **DynamicWeb** (gold) — https://dynamicweb.com/
- **IAMCP** — International Association of Microsoft Channel Partners — https://www.iamcp.org/

Sponsorship enquiries: it@mymail.sim.edu.sg

## Hackathon Timeline (key milestones)
- 16 Jul 2026 — Registration deadline (23:59 SGT)
- 24 Jul 2026 — Opening ceremony, hacking begins (SIM Campus)
- 24–25 Jul 2026 — Hacking period
- 25 Jul 2026 — Submission deadline (12:00 PM SGT)
- 25 Jul 2026 — Judging and winners announced

Full day-by-day schedule to be confirmed on Telegram.

## Who Should Join
Curious students who want to build agentic products — web developers, AI engineers, designers, product managers, business analysts, and anyone willing to learn. All SIM students welcome; cross-university teammates allowed.

## Past Events
- **HackXperience 2025** — 90+ participants, 20 projects. Winner of SIM Outstanding Event Award (Silver, 2025 SLAAN Award). Challenge tracks: DynamicWeb & Kitchen Copilot.
- **Omnitool Hackathon** — 95 participants, 20–21 projects.

## About SIM IT Club
SIM IT Club (ITC) is a student-led organisation under SIM Student Life. Mission: create safe spaces for students to learn technology, build projects, and grow as a community of builders. Tagline: "Your Arrow into Tech."

Programs:
- **ITCamp: Frontendamentals** — 3-month weekly web development bootcamp (HTML, CSS, Tailwind, React, Next.js, Vercel)
- **Industry Workshops** — guest-led sessions with tech professionals
- **Internal Projects** — committee members build and ship real products
- **HackXperience** — annual hackathon open to SIM students

## Pages
- Homepage: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app
- Photo gallery (past events): https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app/gallery
- Project submission portal: https://hackxperience2026.vercel.app/submit

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Registration
Q: How do I register for HackXperience 2026?
A: Your team's Team Leader submits the Team Registration Form (https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/BjC48ET3vW) on behalf of the team. Registration closes 16 July 2026, 23:59 SGT.

Q: What is the team size?
A: 3 to 4 members. One Team Leader is the primary contact and submits the form.

Q: I'm solo or don't have a full team yet.
A: Fill out the Looking for a Team form (https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/Lycc7m85fx) and find teammates during pre-events.

Q: Can I team up with students from other universities?
A: Yes. Indicate SIM vs other institution for each member on the registration form.

Q: Is there a registration fee?
A: No. Free to participate.

Q: Who is eligible?
A: All SIM students, all years. Cross-university teammates allowed.

### Pre-Events
Q: What are the pre-events?
A: Microsoft Foundry Workshop (17 Jul 2026, 7–10 PM, SIM Campus). Join Telegram for updates: https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl

Q: Do I need to attend pre-events?
A: No, but highly recommended.

### Main Event
Q: When and where is HackXperience 2026?
A: 24–25 July 2026 on SIM Campus. Venue details and full schedule via Telegram.

Q: What are the tracks?
A: AI for Living — Care Forward (wellbeing) or Friction To Flow (productivity/automation). Six sub-tracks total; pick one sub-track at submission.

Q: How will projects be judged?
A: Industry judges evaluate track prizes, sponsor choice awards (Microsoft Stack, Entrepreneurial), and community voting. See Prizes section for criteria.

### General
Q: What is HackXperience?
A: SIM IT Club's annual hackathon. 2026 is a 2-day agentic edition. 2025 had 90+ participants, 20 projects, and won SIM Outstanding Event Award (Silver).

Q: How many participants are expected?
A: 100+ students.

Q: Who can I contact?
A: it@mymail.sim.edu.sg, SIM ITCommunity Telegram (https://t.me/+o_3QtjEFmNFhYmFl), or @FukutaroJFS on Telegram.
